metrics.title: AWS Margin
metrics.label: Q4 2025 — +24% YoY; re-acceleration to 29–30% expected FY 2026

moat.description: Dominant scale, switching costs (Prime), and cost advantage (Logistics). AWS backlog hits $244B+, up 40%+ YoY. Amazon has now committed $33B total to Anthropic ($8B prior + $25B new deal at $380B valuation, announced April 20, 2026) — and Anthropic in turn committed $100B+ in AWS spend over the next 10 years, with 5 GW of capacity additions and access to Trainium2 through Trainium4. This bilateral lock-in (Amazon funds Anthropic; Anthropic is Anthropic's primary cloud) is structurally unmatched by any competitor. Anthropic Mythos and Project Glasswing run on AWS Bedrock. Competitive pressure from Azure (+39% YoY) and GCP (+36% YoY) is a monitored risk but the Anthropic $100B commitment provides a 10-year AWS demand floor.
moat.analysisSummary: Amazon possesses a **Wide Economic Moat** driven by four primary pillars:

analysisPoints.title: Cost Advantage
analysisPoints.text: Its massive fulfillment infrastructure creates unit costs that no competitor can match, allowing for faster delivery and lower prices.

analysisPoints.title: Switching Costs
analysisPoints.text: The Prime ecosystem locks in consumers. Once a household is integrated into Prime, the convenience makes shopping elsewhere a "costly" friction.

analysisPoints.title: Network Effect
analysisPoints.text: The 3rd party marketplace creates a flywheel where more sellers attract more buyers, which attracts more sellers.

analysisPoints.title: AWS as the Primary AI Training Cloud
analysisPoints.text: Amazon has now invested $33B total in Anthropic ($8B prior + $25B new, April 20, 2026) — locking in AWS as the exclusive primary cloud for training and deploying Claude models at a $380B Anthropic valuation. Crucially, Anthropic committed to spending $100B+ on AWS technologies over the next 10 years, adding 5 GW of capacity and using Trainium2 through Trainium4 (including future chip generations). This is a bilateral strategic lock-in: Amazon funds Anthropic's growth; Anthropic is structurally dependent on AWS for every Claude API call, Bedrock workload, and frontier model training run. Anthropic's $30B+ annualised revenue (up from $9B at end-2025) is entirely routed through AWS infrastructure — making Anthropic the single fastest-growing AWS revenue driver. The $100B commitment provides a 10-year structural demand floor for AWS that no competing cloud can replicate.

growth.description: AWS re-accelerated to 24% in Q4 2025 — fastest growth in 13 quarters — with a $244B+ backlog (+40% YoY). Oppenheimer and Morgan Stanley expect AWS to re-accelerate toward 29–30% for full-year 2026, driven by enterprise AI migration and Bedrock adoption. The $25B additional Anthropic investment (April 2026, total $33B) brings Anthropic's $100B+ AWS commitment over 10 years — a structural demand floor. Advertising grew 23% to $21.3B in Q4; Q1 2026 revenue consensus is $177.2B (+13% YoY), EPS $1.63. Goldman Sachs raised PT to $290. Tariff headwinds — Chinese seller strain from 125–145% tariffs — are a manageable headwind offset by Amazon's logistics advantage.

additionalNote.title: Financial Outlook
points: AWS growth re-accelerated to 24% in Q4 2025, highest rate in 13 quarters; $244B+ backlog up 40%+ YoY. Oppenheimer/Morgan Stanley forecast AWS re-acceleration to 29–30% for FY 2026.
Advertising revenue reached $21.3B (+22% YoY Q4), cementing Amazon as a top-3 digital ad platform; Goldman Sachs sees advertising margins becoming a 'second engine' for earnings growth.

Amazon committed $33B total to Anthropic ($8B prior + $25B new at $380B valuation, April 2026); Anthropic committed $100B+ in AWS spend over 10 years with 5 GW capacity additions — the largest bilateral AI infrastructure lock-in in cloud history.

Tariff headwinds persist (125–145% on Chinese goods, de minimis eliminated); Chinese sellers (~50%+ of marketplace) raising prices or reducing listings. Amazon's FBA fuel surcharge (3.5%, Apr 17) partially offsets logistics cost inflation. Bill Ackman's Pershing Square disclosed a significant AMZN position in April 2026.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 80 (15–30% blended CAGR) + 5 recurring revenue (AWS contracts + Prime) + 5 TAM expansion (AI inference + sovereign cloud) − 2 Azure/GCP competitive acceleration (39%/36% vs AWS 24%) + 1 Anthropic Mythos driving Bedrock demand − 2 tariff/marketplace headwinds (Chinese sellers) = 87

drivers.name: AWS Cloud
drivers.metric: +24% YoY (Q4 2025), $244B+ backlog (+40% YoY); FY 2026 re-acceleration to 29–30% expected

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: Azure (+39% YoY) and GCP (+36% YoY) are already growing materially faster than AWS (~24%); if this gap widens and Azure captures the majority of new enterprise AI inference workloads through 2026, AWS growth decelerates below 18%, the $200B 2026 capex cycle proves premature, and FCF compression extends to 2028+

valuation.description: Stock at ~$264 sits ~2% below our base fair value of $270 — the margin of safety has compressed materially from the prior $213 entry point. The $25B additional Anthropic investment (April 2026, $100B AWS commitment over 10 years) is a structural catalyst not fully priced in; Goldman Sachs raised its PT to $290. Bull case remains $330 if AWS re-accelerates above 30% as Oppenheimer/Morgan Stanley expect. Key near-term catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings (April 29) — the read on whether AWS holds or exceeds 24% growth and confirms the acceleration thesis. At current prices, risk/reward is balanced; new positions should await a pullback or post-earnings confirmation.

valuationNote.text: Using a 10-year DCF with a 9% WACC and a 3.5% terminal growth rate, our fair value estimate for AMZN is

peAnalysis.summary: At ~32× forward earnings, AMZN now trades at a meaningful premium to the megacap peer median (~23×), reflecting the AWS AI acceleration thesis and Anthropic lock-in. The PEG of 1.80× is firmly in 'premium; requires execution' territory — the multiple is pricing in AWS re-acceleration to 29–30% and the $100B Anthropic AWS commitment materialising. The compression from 36.8× trailing to 32.4× forward signals a significant earnings ramp expectation through 2026–2027, contingent on the $200B capex cycle beginning to monetise.

bear.description: Persistent 125–145% tariffs on Chinese goods cause Chinese sellers (~50%+ of marketplace) to reduce listings and raise prices, disrupting the marketplace flywheel. The $200B capex cycle extends FCF compression while AWS decelerates as Azure and GCP win disproportionate enterprise AI inference workloads.
points: FBA fuel surcharge (3.5%, effective Apr 17) and rate increases squeeze third-party sellers; Chinese sellers (~50%+ of marketplace GMV) reduce listings or raise prices 20–30%, eroding Amazon's low-price competitive advantage and consumer traffic

AWS decelerates toward low-teens % as Azure (+39% YoY) and Google Cloud (+36% YoY) continue gaining enterprise AI inference share; the $100B Anthropic AWS commitment is insufficient to offset broader enterprise switching; AWS market share erodes below 28%
$200B capex drives FCF near-zero for 2026–2027; Q1 2026 earnings disappoint on operating income, the multiple compresses to 22–24× forward earnings, and stock retraces to $170 bear case

base.description: The $200B capex cycle begins monetising by late 2026 — AWS sustains 20%+ growth anchored by the $244B backlog, Anthropic's $30B+ run-rate revenue accelerates Bedrock adoption, advertising scales past $90B annualised, and FCF rebounds above $50B by 2027.
points: AWS maintains 20–25% growth with the $244B backlog (+40% YoY) providing 12+ months of demand visibility; 35%+ margins hold as Bedrock enterprise customers (60% Q/Q spend growth) ramp workloads, amplified by Anthropic's $30B+ annualised revenue all routing through AWS infrastructure
Advertising sustains 20%+ growth as Amazon's intent-based targeting delivers highest-ROI per dollar for third-party sellers, reaching $90B+ annualised run rate by end of 2026
FCF rebounds above $50B by 2027 as the capex cycle matures; seller cost headwinds (fuel surcharge, FBA rate hikes) are absorbed as marketplace GMV recovers in H2 2026

bull.description: AWS re-accelerates above 30% as sovereign AI, enterprise inference, and Anthropic's explosive growth (Mythos model, Project Glasswing) concentrate workloads on the platform, Trainium chips establish a durable silicon moat, and the capex cycle delivers transformational FCF by 2028.
points: AWS growth re-accelerates above 30% as sovereign AI demand + Bedrock enterprise adoption + Anthropic's $100B AWS commitment (5 GW capacity, Trainium2–4) drive the $244B+ backlog to $350B+ by end of 2026; Anthropic's $30B+ run rate scales to $60B+, making it the single largest AWS revenue driver

Trainium3/4 chips achieve cost-performance parity with Nvidia H100/B200 for inference at scale — AWS silicon (Graviton + Trainium) at $10B+ annualised run rate growing triple digits — creating a 2–3 year lead-time moat
FCF surpasses $80B by 2027–2028 as capex transitions to depreciation tailwinds; a capital return programme (buybacks) is announced, triggering a re-rating toward 35× forward earnings

learnedInterfaces.note: Alexa voice interface and AWS console are table stakes easily replicated; not a durable differentiator.

businessLogic.note: AI is commoditizing logistics routing and retail recommendation logic that once required years to build.

publicDataAccess.note: Amazon's 200M+ product reviews and buyer behaviour signals still provide meaningful aggregation advantages, but AI scraping and competitor datasets have eroded the edge. The moat persists in scale, not exclusivity.

talentScarcity.note: AWS cloud operations require fewer skilled human operators thanks to AI-powered automation.

bundling.note: Prime bundle (shipping + video + music + Alexa + Pharmacy + Gaming) is deeply differentiated and drives 200M+ loyalty.

proprietaryData.note: Purchase intent data, AWS usage telemetry, and last-mile logistics operational data are genuinely irreplaceable. AWS $244B+ backlog reflects deep customer commitment. The Anthropic bilateral lock-in ($33B Amazon investment; $100B+ Anthropic AWS commitment over 10 years) creates a proprietary data feedback loop: Anthropic's training workloads on Trainium2–4 generate silicon telemetry no competitor can observe, compounding AWS's custom chip advantage. This is now a structural, not just financial, relationship.

regulatoryLockIn.note: AWS GovCloud, DoD JEDI, HIPAA, FedRAMP, and financial services compliance create enormous switching friction.

networkEffects.note: Marketplace two-sided flywheel: more buyers → more sellers → better selection → more buyers. Self-reinforcing.

transactionEmbedding.note: 1-click purchasing habits, Prime subscription, and AWS embedded in the infrastructure of the global internet.

systemOfRecord.note: AWS is the system of record for global cloud infrastructure; $142B annualized run rate and S3 stores more data than any competitor by a wide margin.

tenMoats.verdict: Amazon's moats are overwhelmingly AI-resilient: the $244B AWS backlog, Trainium custom silicon, proprietary purchase data, and the marketplace flywheel are core strengths that are strengthening — not weakening — in the AI era.
